headline: EasyJet Has Private Equity Firms Going Out of Their Comfort Zone
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The aviation industry has seen plenty of deals activity in recent years, but the battle between two US funds for EasyJet Plc stands out because the volatile, low-margin airlines business rarely draws financial suitors.Most Read from BloombergGates Heir’s Shopping App Claimed Sales It Didn’t DriveApple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft in Pivotal CasePrabowo’s Clampdown on Indonesian Tycoons Fuels Capital FlightRyanair Passenger Partly Sucked From Jet After Window BreaksMicrosoft’

headline: Private Credit Is Coming to 401(k) Plans. These Are the Alternative Asset Managers Set to Cash In.
summary: The private credit sector is growing rapidly, and it could get a huge boost when the average investor can buy in, too.
